title: A Breakthrough in Communication
story: Helen Keller's world changed forever on April 5, 1887. Born blind and deaf, the six-year-old struggled to understand her surroundings. That's when Anne Sullivan, her teacher, had a brilliant idea. She held Helen's hand under a water pump and spelled 'w-a-t-e-r' in her palm. Suddenly, Helen understood! This moment sparked her incredible journey of learning to read, write, and even speak. She went on to graduate from college, becoming an inspiration to millions.
activity_question: What word did Anne Sullivan teach Helen Keller using the water pump?
follow_up_question: How do you think Helen Keller's life changed after learning to communicate?

title: Gandhi's Salty Protest Shakes British Rule
story: Gandhi led a daring protest against unfair British laws in India. After walking nearly 200 miles, he and his followers reached the sea at Dandi on April 5, 1930. title: From Slave to Congressman: Robert Smalls
story: Robert Smalls was born into slavery in 1839, but he didn't let that stop him from achieving greatness. Through courage and determination, he escaped slavery and later became a respected leader. In 1875, Smalls made history by becoming one of the first African-American congressmen for South Carolina. He worked hard to improve education, helping create the state's public school system. Smalls served in Congress until 1887, leaving a lasting impact on his home state.

title: The Silver Bible Heist
story: Thieves pulled off a daring daylight robbery on April 5, 1995. Their target? The Codex Argenteus, or Silver Bible, from Uppsala University Library in Sweden. This ancient book, written in the Gothic language, dates back to the 5th century. Luckily, the precious pages were recovered about a month later, preserving this unique piece of history.

title: Dam Burst Sparks Massive Evacuation
story: Rushing waters roared through Orsk, Russia when a dam on the Ural River suddenly burst on April 5. Thousands of people had to quickly leave their homes as the flood spread. The emergency even reached nearby areas of Kazakhstan. Brave rescue teams worked hard to keep everyone safe during this unexpected event.
activity_question: What caused thousands of people to evacuate?
follow_up_question: Why is it important to have emergency plans for natural disasters?

title: Helping Hands from the Sky
story: Planes roared overhead, dropping vital supplies to people in need. On April 5, 1991, the United States began air drops to help Kurdish refugees in Northern Iraq. Food, water, and medicine floated down on parachutes, bringing hope to those who had fled their homes. This mission showed how countries can work together to support people facing tough times.
activity_question: What did the United States drop from planes to help Kurdish refugees?
follow_up_question: Why do you think air drops were used instead of ground delivery?

title: Canadian Heroes Free Dutch City
story: Cheers erupted as Canadian soldiers rolled into Almelo in 1945. The brave troops of the Second Canadian Corps had battled their way across Europe. Now, on April 5, they liberated this Dutch city from Nazi control. Families rushed to greet their rescuers with hugs and flowers. It was a joyous moment of freedom after years of occupation.
activity_question: Who liberated the Dutch city of Almelo?
follow_up_question: How do you think the people of Almelo felt when they were freed?

title: A Green Gift for All
story: Imagine a world without public parks! In 1847, Birkenhead Park in England changed everything. Designed by Joseph Paxton, it opened its gates on April 5, becoming the first civic public park. This green oasis wasn't just for the wealthy - it welcomed everyone to enjoy nature, fresh air, and relaxation right in the heart of the city.
activity_question: Who designed Birkenhead Park?
bonus_fact: Birkenhead Park inspired the design of Central Park in New York City.
